project.title: Identification of E2f1 interactors during early embryonic development in Xenopus tropicalis
project.projectDescription: In this project, we performed E2f1 immunoprecipitation followed by mass spectrometry analysis to identify its interacting partners during early embryonic development in Xenopus tropicalis.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Xenopus tropicalis embryos were injected with HA-tagged E2f1 mRNA at one cell stage. At stage 8, were collected and homogenized in lysis buffer (20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 150 nM NaCl, 1 mM EDTA, 1% Triton, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM PMSF, protease inhibitor cocktail (Roche, 04693132001), 1 mM NaVO4). Protein lysates were then mixed with 2 volumes of 1,1,2-Trichlorotrifluoroethane. After centrifugation, the protein lysates at the upper layer were collected to measure concentrations using the BCA assay (Beyotime, P0011). The E2f1-associated proteins were immunoprecipitated with Protein A/G magnetic beads (MCE, HY-K0202) coupled with HA antibody (Sigma, H6908) for 4 hr. After washing with TBST, an overnight on-beads digestion was carried out with sequencing-grade trypsin (Promega, V5111). Afterwards, the samples were analyzed on an LTQ-Orbitrap Elite mass spectrometer system (Thermo).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Mass spectrometry data was processed by MaxQuant for label-free quantification with “match between run” function activated and database searching was against the UniProt X. tropicalis database.

Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: top-speed mode with a 3 s cycle time
Data-dependent MS/MS scans were performed in the ion trap using an isolation window of 1.6 m/z
Comment[GradientTime]: effective gradient was 7–22% buffer B over 50 min, followed by an increase to 35% B over 10 min
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: on-bead digestion was performed overnight with sequencing-grade trypsin (Promega, V5111)
